## Contract

**Delegator use only.** Any agent that launches subagents reads this registry to resolve compact rules, then injects matching rule text into subagent prompts under `## Project Standards (auto-resolved)`.

Subagents still read their assigned executor/phase skill. During normal runtime, they do **not** independently discover or load additional project/user `SKILL.md` files or this registry; project/user rules arrive pre-digested. Explicit fallback loading is degraded self-healing and must be reported in `skill_resolution` as `fallback-registry` or `fallback-path`.

## Project Standards (mundo-limpio-backend)

Files scanned:
- `~/.config/opencode/skills/mundo-limpio-backend/SKILL.md`

### Stack & Conventions

- Java 21, Spring Boot 3.3.0, Maven, PostgreSQL, Flyway, JWT (jjwt 0.12.5)
- Clean Architecture en capas por módulo: domain → repository → service → controller → dto → mapper
- Comentarios en español con formato WHAT/WHY/DIFFERENCES
- Conventional commits en inglés: `type(scope): description`
- Branches: `feat/<desc>` o `fix/<desc>` desde develop
- Strict TDD activo: tests first, RED → GREEN → REFACTOR
- No atribución AI en commits (no "Co-Authored-By")
- SDD ciclo completo: explore → propose → specs → design → tasks → apply → verify → archive
- Artifact store: openspec (config.yaml + changes/ + specs/)

### Testing

- `mvn clean verify` como comando build + test + cobertura
- Unit: JUnit 5 + Mockito + AssertJ
- Integration: Testcontainers PostgreSQL
- Coverage: JaCoCo 0.8.12, umbral 0.20
- Testcontainers requiere DOCKER_HOST=tcp://localhost:2375 en Windows

## Selected skills and compact rules

### mundo-limpio-backend
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\mundo-limpio-backend\SKILL.md
- Trigger: mundo-limpio-backend, Java backend, Spring Boot backend, backend development, migration
- Rules:
  - Stack: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.3.0, PostgreSQL, Flyway, JWT (jjwt 0.12.5), RBAC ADMIN/OPERATOR
  - Arquitectura: capas por módulo domain → repository → service → controller → dto → mapper
  - SDD obligatorio: ciclo completo fase por fase, sin saltos
  - Strict TDD activo: tests primero, RED → GREEN → REFACTOR
  - Comentarios en español con WHAT/WHY/DIFFERENCES
  - Conventional commits en inglés `type(scope): description`
  - Branches: `feat/<desc>` o `fix/<desc>` desde develop, stacked PRs a develop
  - `mvn clean verify` comando build+test
  - No AI attribution en commits

### sdd-propose
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-propose\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD proposal phase
- Rules:
  - Leer proposal existente antes de actualizar; no sobrescribir ciegamente
  - Toda proposal DEBE tener rollback plan y success criteria
  - Size budget: <450 words; usar bullets y tablas, no prosa
  - Capabilities section es el contrato con sdd-spec — llenar siempre
  - Persistir artefacto según mode (openspec: archivo, engram: mem_save)

### sdd-spec
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-spec\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD spec writing phase
- Rules:
  - New capabilities → full spec nuevo en openspec/specs/<name>/spec.md
  - Modified capabilities → delta spec en change folder
  - Usar Given/When/Then para escenarios
  - Usar RFC 2119: MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY
  - Specs describen WHAT, no HOW

### sdd-design
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-design\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD technical design phase
- Rules:
  - Leer codebase real antes de diseñar; nunca adivinar
  - Toda decisión DEBE tener rationale (alternative considered + why chosen)
  - File changes con paths concretos del proyecto, no abstractos
  - Seguir patrones existentes del proyecto aunque no sean "best practice"
  - Size budget: <800 words
  - Testing strategy: especificar unit vs integration

### sdd-tasks
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-tasks\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD task breakdown phase
- Rules:
  - Leer proposal + spec + design antes de crear tasks
  - Agrupar tasks por fase con numeración jerárquica (1.1, 1.2, ...)
  - Tasks individuales completables en una sesión
  - Incluir Review Workload Forecast con budget de 400 líneas
  - Separar tareas de implementación en slices para chained PRs si aplica

### sdd-apply
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-apply\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD implementation phase
- Rules:
  - Strict TDD: Safety Net → RED → GREEN → TRIANGULATE → REFACTOR
  - Ejecutar solo archivo de test relevante durante ciclo TDD (no suite completa)
  - No escribir código de producción sin test fallido primero
  - Triangulación obligatoria (≥2 casos) excepto tareas puramente estructurales
  - Assertions DEBEN verificar comportamiento real, no trivial
  - Safety Net antes de modificar archivos existentes

### sdd-verify
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-verify\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD verification phase
- Rules:
  - Ejecutar tests; análisis estático solo nunca es verificación
  - Comparar spec primero, design segundo, task completion tercero
  - Spec scenario sin test que lo cubra → CRITICAL
  - No corregir bugs; solo reportarlos
  - Strict TDD activo → cargar strict-tdd-verify.md

### sdd-archive
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-archive\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD archive phase
- Rules:
  - Mover change folder a archive/ con prefijo YYYY-MM-DD-
  - Mergear delta specs a main specs en openspec/specs/
  - No modificar ni borrar archived changes (audit trail)
  - Warning antes de mergear destructive deltas

### sdd-explore
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-explore\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD exploration phase
- Rules:
  - Investigar requerimientos antes de proponer cambios
  - Explorar alternativas técnicas y riesgos
  - Output opcional: exploration.md en change folder

### branch-pr
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\branch-pr\SKILL.md
- Trigger: creating, opening, or preparing PRs for review
- Rules:
  - Branch naming: `^(feat|fix|chore|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|revert)\/[a-z0-9._-]+$`
  - Conventional commits en inglés: `type(scope): description`
  - PR template obligatorio con linked issue, type label, summary, changes, test plan
  - No AI attribution en commits

### chained-pr
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\chained-pr\SKILL.md
- Trigger: PRs over 400 lines, stacked PRs, review slices
- Rules:
  - Split PRs >400 lines a menos que maintainer acepte `size:exception`
  - Mantener cada PR reviewable en ≤60 min
  - Incluir dependency diagram marcando PR actual con 📍
  - State start, end, prior dependencies, follow-up en cada child PR
  - No mezclar chain strategies después de elegir una

### work-unit-commits
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\work-unit-commits\SKILL.md
- Trigger: implementation, commit splitting, chained PRs
- Rules:
  - Commit = deliverable behavior unit, no por tipo de archivo
  - Tests y docs van con el código que verifican/explican
  - Cada commit debe contar una historia entendible en diff + message
  - Si SDD forecast >400 líneas, agrupar en chained PR slices antes de implementar
  - Rollback factible sin revertir trabajo no relacionado

### sdd-init
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-init\SKILL.md
- Trigger: sdd init, iniciar sdd, openspec init
- Rules:
  - Detectar stack real, convenciones, herramientas, persistence mode — nunca adivinar
  - OpenSpec mode: crear openspec/config.yaml con context, testing, rules
  - Construir/actualizar .atl/skill-registry.md con compact rules
  - Persistir testing capabilities y project context
  - strict_tdd: true si hay test runner; false si no

### sdd-onboard
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\sdd-onboard\SKILL.md
- Trigger: SDD onboarding for full cycle walkthrough
- Rules:
  - Walk through SDD cycle on real codebase
  - One phase at a time, ask before proceeding
  - Explain each phase's purpose and artifacts

### cognitive-doc-design
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\cognitive-doc-design\SKILL.md
- Trigger: writing guides, READMEs, RFCs, onboarding, architecture docs
- Rules:
  - Diseñar docs para reducir carga cognitiva del lector
  - Cada doc debe responder: qué, por qué, cómo, cuándo usar
  - Anticipar preguntas del lector y responderlas

### comment-writer
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\comment-writer\SKILL.md
- Trigger: PR feedback, issue replies, reviews, collaboration comments
- Rules:
  - Warm, direct tone
  - Validate question makes sense before answering
  - Explain WHY technically, not just what

### judgment-day
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\judgment-day\SKILL.md
- Trigger: dual review, adversarial review, judging
- Rules:
  - Blind dual review: evaluate without knowing the other reviewer's opinion
  - Fix confirmed issues only
  - Re-judge after fixes

### issue-creation
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\issue-creation\SKILL.md
- Trigger: creating GitHub issues, bug reports, feature requests
- Rules:
  - Issue-first: verify issue exists and is approved before PR
  - Include reproduction steps for bugs
  - Clear acceptance criteria for features

### skill-creator
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.claude\skills\skill-creator\SKILL.md
- Trigger: new skills, agent instructions, documenting AI usage patterns
- Rules:
  - LLM-first: frontmatter + Activation Contract + Hard Rules + Decision Gates + Execution Steps + Output Contract + References
  - Hard Rules son observables, no opiniones
  - Output Contract especifica exactamente qué devolver
  - References deben ser locales y estables

### teaching-mode
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\teaching-mode\SKILL.md
- Trigger: user asking for explanations ("enseñame", "explícame", "por qué", "how does this work")
- Rules:
  - Explicar cada decisión técnica con fundamento
  - Incluir alternativas y tradeoffs
  - Usar analogías del mundo real cuando clarifiquen

### tutorial-output
- Path: C:\Users\zottt\.config\opencode\skills\tutorial-output\SKILL.md
- Trigger: completing significant task, fix, investigation, or SDD phase
- Rules:
  - Crear .md tutorial explicando reasoning, decisions, alternatives
  - Step-by-step process para que el usuario aprenda
